assertions.criterion: The answer presents task shape → executor → target surface → effect floor → exact qualification → same-floor ranking/fallback → real handle → endpoint verification in that output order.
Executor responsibility is kept separate from target harness/surface and current origin is not treated as the worker-pool boundary.

The floor includes a rationale derived solely from task shape, risk, and error cost before qualification or ranking; a generic target_surface output field before effect_floor is the required record order and is not target-driven floor selection.
Candidate is not treated as certified and case-applicable exact qualification or admission unknowns fail closed.
Ranking and fallback consider only already-qualified candidates at the same effect floor; tight capacity never silently lowers the original floor.
For workflow cases, planning semantics are separated from runtime availability and Codex does not invent the Claude Workflow runtime; non-workflow cases pass as not applicable.
A real recon-able handle is required before in_flight; no handle is honest when dispatch is blocked.
Runtime terminal, external closed, CI green, or self-report is not task done; endpoint verification gates closure, while a never-dispatched case honestly reports endpoint not reached.
No dynamic provider/model/quota catalog or provider flags are invented from memory.
files_opened_in_order shows at most one reference drill after the master skill.

success_contract.with_hub: Every case passes A1-A10 and opens no more than one reference after the entry skill.
success_contract.comparison: Each paired without-hub run is retained; uplift is supported when the with-hub run passes and the paired baseline misses a load-bearing assertion or exceeds one drill.
